original: Boniface II begins his reign as Catholic Pope on the death of Pope Felix IV
Discorus begins his reign as Catholic anti-Pope
St Felix IV reign as Catholic Pope ends
Lithuanians and Semigallians defeat the Livonian Brothers of the Sword in the Battle of Šiauliai
Switzerland becomes an independent state
Settled engagement of Karel of Luxembourg & Claudia the Beaujeu

Remaining crew of Spanish Narváez Expedition depart Florida in 5 crude boats destined for Texas, by sailing along the coast, a course disrupted by storms that carry them out to open Gulf of Mexico waters; boats land on barrier islands in early November
Cardinal Thomas Wolsey stripped of office as Lord Chancellor of England
Battle at Zutphen: Dutch army, assisted by the English, against the Spanish
All-female jury hears the case of Judith Catchpole, accused of killing her child, and acquits her in Patuxent County, Maryland
People of Rotterdam strike over the high cost of butter
French troops occupy Rio de Janeiro
King of Poland Stanisław I [Leszczyński] flees to Danzig
Pangeran Gusti installed as Sultan of Banten
Nassau Hall opens at Princeton University
1st Partition of Poland is ratified by Austria, Prussia and Russia
Russian trappers establish a colony on Kodiak Island, Alaska
Losantville, Ohio (now Cincinnati) founded by land speculators Mathias Denman and Colonel Robert Patterson, and surveyor John Filson; the first settlers arrive in  December
US Congress passes act requiring the first Postmaster General to report to the President through the Secretary of the Treasury
Day one of the short-loved French Republican calendar (declared 1793, abandoned 1806)
City of Des Moines, Iowa, incorporated as Fort Des Moines

Federal troops shoot and kill 12 Navajo men, women and children, and wound 40 more, following a dispute over a friendly horse race during monthly "Ration Day" at Fort Fauntleroy in Bear Springs. Territory of New Mexico
Battle of Fisher's Hill, Virginia - Confederate General Jubal Early Retreats to Brown's Gap after advance by Union army under General Philip Sheridan
Race riots in New Orleans, Louisiana
Lord Randolph Churchill makes a speech in Ulster in opposition to Home Rule e.g. "Ulster will fight and Ulster will be right"
First known ascent of Mt. Olympus, Washington, by an expedition led by Joseph P. O'Neil
Race riot in Atlanta Georgia  kill 10 black people and 2 white
In New Zealand Domestic workers call for a 68-hour working week
Race riot in Atlanta Georgia, kills 21
Bulgaria declares independence from the Ottoman Empire (Turkey)
Saskatchewan Rugby Football Union forms with an initial 9 team competition
The Duke of York's Cinema opens in Brighton. It is still operating today as the oldest continually operating cinema in Britain
The first batch of Indian passive resisters, consisting of 12 men and 4 women (including Mrs. Kasturba Gandhi) are arrested at Volksrust and imprisoned in Pietermaritzburg, South Africa
WWI: German submarine U-9 sinks three British armored cruisers - HMS Aboukir, HMS Cressy, and HMS Hogue, in the southern North Sea, resulting in 1,459 deaths
Southern Methodist University  in Dallas, Texas, holds its 1st class
Xavier University, 1st Black Catholic College in US, opens in NO LA
Steel strike begins in the US and continues for four months
Chicago grand jury convenes to investigate charges that eight White Sox players conspired to fix the 1919 World Series
The League of Nations accepts membership of independent Estonia, Latvia, and Lithuania
US Congress passes the Cable Act, under which an American women who marries an 'alien' will not lose citizenship; neither will a women marrying an American automatically become a citizen
New York left fielder Ben Paschal hits 2 inside-the-park-home runs as the Yankees score an 11-6 home victory over Chicago White Sox
Belgian crown prince Leopold and Swedish Princess Astrid get engaged
New York Yankees Earle Coombs hits 3 triples
Coalition Government forms in New Zealand to combat the Depression (their lack of success will lead to the election of the First Labour Government in 1935)
Hungarian government of Gyula Károlyi falls
An explosion takes place at Gresford Colliery in Wales, leading to the deaths of 266 miners and rescuers
MLB Boston Braves lose NL then record 110th game of year en route to 115
Date celebrated as the first International Hobbit Day and the birthdays of Bilbo and Frodo Baggins
Forest fire kills 14 and injures 50 in Cody, Wyoming
Spanish Civil War: Peña Blanca is taken, ending the Battle of El Mazuco
British signal officer Bill Hudson lands in Montenegro
British dwarf submarines attack German battleship Tirpitz
Destroyer HMS Itchen torpedoed and sinks
Destroyer HMS Keppel sinks U-229
Boulogne reoccupied by the Allies
US troops land on Ulithi atoll, western Pacific
Canadian woman Evelyn Dick charged with butchering her husband in Hamilton, Ontario
Brooklyn Dodgers clinch NL pennant as Chicago defeats St. Louis Cardinals in second game of a double header
WFMY TV channel 2 in Greensboro-High Point, NC (CBS) 1st broadcast
Islamic uprising in Atjeh, Indonesia
Brooklyn Dodger pitcher Karl Spooner strikes out 15 NY Giants in his 1st MLB game
-28] Hurricane Janet, kills 500 in the Caribbean
On BBC radio Grace Archer is killed in a barn fire on Brookfield Farm - the BBC denies this was to spoil the launch of rival network ITV
KTVK TV channel 3 in Phoenix, AZ (ABC) begins broadcasting
The first Romanian production ofGeorge Enescu's 1936 opera "Œdipe", premieres in Bucharest, with original French libretto translated into Romanian
US nuclear submarine USS Skate remains 31 days under the Pole (record)
Chicago White Sox clinch AL pennant
Mali (formerly French Sudan) declares independence from France
Antonio Albertondo (Argentina) completes 1st "double" crossing swim of English Channel in 43 hrs 10 min at 42.
MLB Baltimore Orioles Jim Gentile ties record of 5 grand slams in a single season
Czechoslovakian premier Viliam Široký deposed by Josef Lenart
Ceasefire between India and Pakistan goes into effect, ending the Indo-Pakistani War
KMEB TV channel 10 in Wailuku, HI (PBS) begins broadcasting
Only 413 show up at a Yankee Stadium game
Orioles beat A's 6-1 to clinch their 1st AL pennant
Surveyor 2 crashes on the Moon, and all contact with it is lost
Phillies release pitcher Dallas Green, their future manager
USSR performs nuclear test at the Semipalatinsk Test Site in northeast Kazakhstan
Iraq adopts constitution
KMTC (now KDEB) TV channel 27 in Springfield, MO (IND) 1st broadcast
Minnesota Twins utility César Tovar pitches a hitless inning & plays all 9 positions in a 2-1 win over Oakland A's at Metropolitan Stadium
"Music Scene" debuts on ABC-TV
China performs nuclear test at Lop Nor, PRC
OPEC directs members to negotiate price increases to offset the devaluation of the US dollar
US performs nuclear test at Nevada Test Site
Baltimore Oriole Al Bumbry hits 3 triples vs Milwaukee Brewers
World Football League folds
Minnesota Twin Bert Blyleven no-hits California Angels, 9-0
West German Red Army Faction terrorist kills policeman in Utrecht
Israeli Prime MinisterMenachem Beginreturns home after Camp David summit
Israel performs nuclear test at Indian Ocean
Montreal Expo Larry Parrish HRs off Phillies Rawly Eastwick in DH
Iraq invades Iran in an attempt to control the Shatt al-Arab waterway
Duleep Mendis completes twin cricket tons for Sri Lanka v India
San Francisco's cable cars made a final run before closing down for 20-month
NASA launches Galaxy-B
USSR performs underground nuclear test
Zhu Jianhau (China) sets high jump record at 7'9 3/4"
37th Emmy Awards: "Cagney & Lacey", "The Cosby Show" and Tyne Daly win
Plaza Accord is signed in New York City by France, West Germany, Japan, UK, and US, to depreciate the US dollar
St Louis Cardinals set an unusual streak record by winning 9 of 10 games, each pitched by a different man
35 countries signs disarmament accord in Stockholm
India v Australia Test Cricket at Madras finishes in a tie
Dow Jones Industrial Average rises record 75.23 points
NFL players go on strike for 24 days (2nd regular-season strike)
American swimmer Janet Evans sets a world record 4:03.85 to win the 400m freestyle gold medal at the Seoul Olympics; 3rd gold medal of the Games (400m I/M, 800m)
Canada begins production of a $5 silver Maple Leaf bullion coin
South Korean coaches attack NZ referee after disputing his decision, Olympic Korean boxer stages a 67 minute sit-in
Soviet gymnast Vladimir Artemov wins individual all-round gold medal at the Seoul Olympics; follows his teams all-round victory; wins 4 gold for the Games
IRA bomb kills 10 Royal Marines bandsmen in Kent
MLB St. Louis Cardinals outfielder Leon Durham suspended for 60 days due to drugs
Saudi Arabia expels many Jordanian and Yemeni diplomats for suspected espionage
California University makes Dead Sea Scrolls public
Dutch banks ABN and AMRO merge
Harry Gant wins NSACAR Goody's 500
NY MTA yanks 6,000 ads for "Dr Tusch" (Dr Jeffrey Lavigne)
Heavy storm in South France, 34 die
A Transair Georgian Airlines Tu-154 is shot down by a missile in Sukhumi, Georgia
STS-51 (Discovery) lands
Ukrainian Prime Minister Leonid Kuchma resigns due to the "slow pace of reform"
Rasmussen government in Denmark resigns
E-3B AWACS crash outside of Elmendorf AFB, Alaska after multiple bird strikes to two of the four engines soon after takeoff; all 24 on board killed
American sportscaster Marv Albert pleads not guilty to sexual assault charges
Bentalha massacre in Algeria; over 200 villagers killed
David Hempleman-Adams becomes the first person to cross the Atlantic Ocean in an open wicker basket hot air balloon
German maglev train collides with a maintenance vehicle near Lathen, Lower Saxony, killing 23 people and injuring 11
The F-14 Tomcat retires from the United States Navy
7 pedestrians are killed by a drunk driver who hit a bus stop at 200km an hour in Moscow
78 people are killed in a church suicide bombing in Peshawar, Pakistan
NASA's MAVEN space probe successfully arrives in orbit over Mars
Palangkaraya in Indonesian Borneo records the highest air pollutant index (API) value ever recorded of 1,986 due to haze caused by forest fires deliberately lit to clear land for palm oil plantations
Volkswagen admits that 11 million cars have been wrongly programmed to appear to emit lesser emissions than they are
Police officer Betty Shelby charged with manslaughter for fatally shooting unarmed black man Terence Crutcher in Tulsa, Oklahoma
Four gunmen open fire on Revolutionary Guard soldiers at a parade in Ahvaz, Iran, killing 25 including civilians
Billy Porter becomes the first openly gay actor to win an Emmy for lead actor in a drama series for "Pose"
America's COVID-19 death toll passes 200,000, more than any other country
PresidentXi Jinpingof China pledges at the UN that the country will adopt stronger climate targets including becoming carbon neutral by 2060
WHO warns urgent action needed on air pollution, is on a par with smoking and a poor diet, causing seven million premature deaths a year
Catholics at 45.7% outnumber Protestants at 43.48% in Northern Ireland for the first time according to the result of the 2021 census
China sets a new world record for the most resistive magnet, at 42.02 tesla, at Hefei Institutes of Physical Science, creating a magnetic field more than 800,000 times stronger than Earth’s
Ecuador begins planned power cuts across the country amid the worst drought in 60 years, which is disrupting its hydroelectric power plants
Sri Lanka elects left-leaning Anura Kumara Dissanayake as their president after a second tally of votes
Roman EmperorNerocreates the Legio I Italica, a legion of soldiers all over six feet tall
Treaty of Blois signed between Philip of Burgundy, Holy RomanEmperor Maximilian Iand French KingLouis XII
Playwright and poetBen Jonsonis arrested and jailed for manslaughter as the result of a duel where he killed actor Gabriel Spenser
Moliere's comedic play "L'amour Medecin" premieres for KingLouis XIVat Versailles
Robert Walpolebecomes the first British "Prime Minister" (actually First Lord of the Treasury) to live at 10 Downing Street
Bonnie Prince Charlie's army returns to Edinburgh
Coronation of KingGeorge IIIand Queen Charlotte of the United Kingdom
Benjamin Franklinpublishes a hoax letter "An Edict by the King of Prussia" in the Public Advertiser, criticizing Britain's colonial policies in the American colonies
French First Republic is formed by the National Convention, stripping the French king of his powers
John Quincy Adamsbecomes US Secretary of State
During his HMS Beagle voyageCharles Darwindiscovers a large number of fossils at Punta Alta in Argentina

US PresidentAbraham Lincolnissues preliminary Emancipation Proclamation, threatening to free all enslaved people in the rebel southern states if those states fail to re-join the Union by January 1, 1863
War of the Triple Alliance: Decisive battle of Curupayty results in a Paraguayan victory and heavy losses for Argentinian and Brazilian forces
Richard Wagner's opera "Das Rheingold" (the first piece of his "Ring" cycle) premieres in Munich, Germany
Rudolf Virchowdelivers an anti-Darwinian speech to the Congress of German Naturalists and Physicians in Munich, where he speaks against the teaching of the theory of evolution in schools
Queen Victoriasurpasses her grandfather KingGeorge IIIas the (then) longest reigning monarch in British history
Boston Rustlers' future Baseball Hall of Fame pitcherCy Youngbeats Pittsburgh Pirates, 1-0 at Forbes Field for his final career victory, number 511
Philadelphia second basemanEddie Collinsbecomes only player in MLB history to steal 6 bases in one game for a second time as A's beat St. Louis Browns, 8-2
George M. Cohan's play "Seven Keys to Baldpate" premieres in NYC

Prime Minister of the Union of South Africa,Louis Botha, assumes command of the armed forces after dismissing General Christiaan Frederik Beyers for his resistance to aid the British in the war against Germany
Music Box Theater opens at 239 W 45th Street, NYC; Built by theatrical producer Sam H. Harris forIrving Berlin's musical revue "The Music Box", written specifically for the venue
"The long count" - in a famous boxing rematch, Gene Tunney beatsJack Dempseyby 10-round unanimous decision at Soldiers Field, Chicago to retain world heavyweight title; crowd 104,943; gate $2,858,660
PresidentJose P. Laureldeclares the Philippines under martial law in 1944 through Proclamation No. 29
Omar Bradleypromoted to rank of 5-star general in the United States Army
Duke Snider's 39th & 40th home runs are last hit at Ebbets Field
Western comedy series "Maverick" premieres on ABC television starringJames Garner
Polish composerKrzysztof Penderecki's "Threnody to the Victims of Hiroshima" premieres at Warsaw Autumn Festival,Andrzej Markowski conducting Kraków Philharmonic
Pete Seegerheadlines 'Hootenanny, at Carnegie Hall' in NYC;Bob Dylanshares the bill
Edward Albee's play "Delicate Balance" premieres in NYC (Pulitzer Prize for Drama 1967)
Ceremony to mark relocation of ancient Egyptian Abu Simbel temples, honoringRamesses IIafter rebuilt 200 meters inland away from Aswan Dam
San Francisco GiantWillie Mays, becomes second player to hit 600 home runs
US PresidentRichard Nixonrequests 1,000 new FBI agents for college campuses
Henry Kissingersworn in as America's 1st Jewish Secretary of State, succeeds William Rogers
Second assassination attempt on US PresidentGerald Fordby Sara Jane Moore fails in San Francisco
TV drama "Charlie's Angels" starringFarrah Fawcett,Kate Jackson,Jaclyn SmithandJohn Forsythedebuts
John LennonandYoko Onosign a recording contract with Geffen Records
TV sitcom "Family Ties" starringMichael J. Foxpremieres on NBC
The Everly Brothers reunite after 10 years apart in concert at the Royal Albert Hall, London
Billy Martin's right arm is broken by pitcher Ed Whitson
LA DodgerFernando Valenzuelais 1st Mexican to win 20 games
Boston Red SoxWade Boggsties AL record of 200 hits for 5 straight seasons
US TV series "Full House" starringBob Sagetand John Stamos debuts
American swimmerMatt Biondiwins the men's 100m freestyle gold medal in Olympic record 48.63 at the Seoul Games; beats countryman Chris Jacobs & Stéphan Caron of France
TV drama "Baywatch" starringDavid HasselhoffandPamela Andersondebuts on NBC
Andre Dawsonsteals his 300th base & is only player other thanWillie Maysto have 300 HRs, 300 steals & 2,000 hits
Miami Dolphins coachDon Shularecords his 300th career NFL victory
Supreme Soviet dismisses PresidentBoris Yeltsin
Texas Ranger playerNolan Ryanat 46, pitches his last game
"Friends" TV sitcom created by David Crane and Marta Kauffman debuts on NBC, starringJennifer Aniston,Courteney Cox,Lisa Kudrow,Matt LeBlanc,Matthew PerryandDavid Schwimmer
Elton Johnreleases "Candle in the Wind 1997", a tribute toDiana, Princess of Walesin the US
"The West Wing" created by Alan Sorkin and starringMartin Sheen, Rob Lowe and Richard Schiff, debuts on NBC
54th Emmy Awards:The West Wing, Friends, Michael Chiklis andAllison Janneywin
TV sitcom "Two and a Half Men" premieres starringCharlie SheenandJon Cryer, created by Chuck Lorre and Lee Aronsohn
"Lost" created byJ.J. Abramsand starring Matthew Fox and Evangeline Lilly debuts on ABC in the US
SF Giants outfielderBarry Bondswas intentionally walked 4 times in a 9-inning game (MLB record) for the second time (1 May 2004)
Teen noir series "Veronica Mars" created by Rob Thomas and starringKristen Bellpremieres on UPN in the US
Hezbollah leaderHassan Nasrallahclaims "Divine Victory" over Israel in a speech to supporters in Beirut after the final Israeli soldiers withdraw from southern Lebanon
Crime drama "NCIS LA" starringChris O'Donnell,LL Cool J, and Daniela Ruah premieres on CBS
65th Prime time Emmy Awards: "Breaking Bad" and "Modern Family" win
Angela Merkeland the Christian Democrats win a third term with their best result since 1990 in German Federal elections
Chinese PresidentXi Jinpingarrives in Seattle to start his first state visit to the US
Pope Francisgreeted byPresident Obamaand Vice PresidentBidenas he arrives at Joint Base Andrews, Maryland, for his 6 day tour of the US
SingerRihannais appointed as an ambassador for Barbados
"Howdy, Modi!" US PresidentDonald Trumpholds a mass rally for Indian PMNarendra Modiin Houston, Texas
71st Emmy Awards: "Fleabag", "Game Of Thrones", "Chernobyl",Phoebe Waller-Bridge, Jharrel Jerome win
Laver Cup Men's Tennis, Geneva:Alexander Zverevbeats Milos Raonic 6-4 3-6 10-4 to earn Team Europe a dramatic 13-11 win over Team World; Europe's 3rd straight win
US PresidentDonald Trumpadmits he spoke to Ukrainian President aboutJoe Biden's son, after news a US intelligence officer made an official complaint about the call
PMBoris Johnsonannounces that the UK has reached a 'perilous turning point' in the pandemic as he announces new restrictions
US PresidentJoe Bidenannounces a military aid package for Ukraine while meeting with the country's president VolodymyrZelenskyyat the White House

The last eight people - Martha Corey, Margaret Scott, Mary Easty, Alice Parker, Ann Pudeator, Wilmott Redd, Samuel Wardwell, and Mary Parker - hanged for allegedly practicing witchcraft as a result of the Salem witch trials in Massachusetts Bay Colony; 19 hanged overall, with six other deaths caused by the hysteria
Commercial television begins in the UK with the launch of ITV, soon airs the 1st advert on UK TV, for Gibbs SR toothpaste
French Prime Minister Laurent Fabius appears on TV to confess "Agents of the DGSE sank this boat [Rainbow Warrior]. They acted on orders.”
